serviceDescription.aws: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and management service that provides data and actionable insights for AWS, hybrid, and on-premises applications and infrastructure resources.
serviceDescription.aws-iso: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and management service that provides data and actionable insights for AWS, hybrid, and on-premises applications and infrastructure resources.
serviceDescription.aws-iso-b: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and management service that provides data and actionable insights for AWS, hybrid, and on-premises applications and infrastructure resources.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and management service that provides data and actionable insights for Amazon Web Services, hybrid, and on-premises applications and infrastructure resources.
serviceDescription.aws-us-gov: Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and management service that provides data and actionable insights for AWS, hybrid, and on-premises applications and infrastructure resources.

cards.title: Metrics
cards.helpText: Metric pricing is prorated by the hour and our calculation assumes customers are sending custom metrics at least once an hour each day of the month.

components.text: The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.label: Number of Metrics (includes detailed and custom metrics)

cards.title: APIs
cards.helpText: You can request up to five statistics for the same metric in a single GetMetricData API request. Additional statistics are billed as an additional metric.

components.text: The calculations below exclude Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Number of other API requests
components.description: GetMetricStatistics, ListMetrics, PutMetricData, GetDashboard, ListDashboards, PutDashboard and DeleteDashboards requests are the other request types which are billed at the same price.

helpText.aws: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs service allows you to collect and store logs from your resources, applications, and services in near real-time. There are three main categories of logs 1) Vended logs. 2) Logs that are published by AWS services. Currently over 30 AWS services publish logs to CloudWatch. 3) Custom logs.
helpText.aws-iso: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs service allows you to collect and store logs from your resources, applications, and services in near real-time. There are three main categories of logs 1) Vended logs. 2) Logs that are published by AWS services. Currently over 30 AWS services publish logs to CloudWatch. 3) Custom logs.
helpText.aws-iso-b: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs service allows you to collect and store logs from your resources, applications, and services in near real-time. There are three main categories of logs 1) Vended logs. 2) Logs that are published by AWS services. Currently over 30 AWS services publish logs to CloudWatch. 3) Custom logs.
helpText.aws-cn: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs service allows you to collect and store logs from your resources, applications, and services in near real-time. There are three main categories of logs 1) Vended logs. 2) Logs that are published by Amazon Web services. Currently over 30 Amazon Web services publish logs to CloudWatch. 3) Custom logs.
helpText.aws-us-gov: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs service allows you to collect and store logs from your resources, applications, and services in near real-time. There are three main categories of logs 1) Vended logs. 2) Logs that are published by AWS services. Currently over 30 AWS services publish logs to CloudWatch. 3) Custom logs.

components.description: Log volume archived is estimated to be 15% of Log volume ingested (due to compression). Storage/Archival costs are estimated assuming customer choses a retention period of one (1) month. Default retention setting is ‘never expire’.

cards.title: Logs Insights Queries (Analyse Log Data)
cards.helpText: Logs Insights enables you to drive actionable intelligence from your logs to address operational issues without needing to provision servers or manage software. You can instantly begin writing queries with aggregations, filters, and regular expressions.

components.label: price for Data Scanned for Logs Insights

operands.variableLabel: price for Data Scanned for Logs Insights

cards.title: Dashboards and Alarms
cards.helpText: Dashboards enable you to create re-usable graphs and visualize your cloud resources and applications in a unified view. Alarms allow you to set a threshold on metrics and trigger an action.

components.text: The calculations for dashboards below include Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Number of Standard Resolution Alarm Metrics
Number of High Resolution Alarm Metrics
Number of composite alarms
components.description: Composite alarms can combine any type of CloudWatch alarm. Metric alarms are billed based on the number of metrics per alarm, while composite alarms are billed per alarm unit.

components.label: Number of alarms defined with a Metrics Insights query
Average number of metrics scanned by each Metrics Insights query

operands.variableLabel: Number of Standard Resolution Alarm Metrics
Number of High Resolution Alarm Metrics
Number of alarms defined with a Metrics Insights query
Average number of metrics scanned by each Metrics Insights query

cards.title: Canaries
cards.helpText: Canaries in CloudWatch Synthetics are configurable scripts that follow the same routes and perform the same actions as a customer. They give you an outside-in view of your customers’ experiences, and your service’s availability from their point of view.

components.text: The calculations below include Free Tier discounts.

components.label: price for Canaries per run tier

components.tieredPricingReferLabel: price for Canaries per run tier

cards.title: Contributor Insights for CloudWatch Logs
cards.helpText: CloudWatch Contributor Insights allows you to create rules to analyze structured log events as they are sent to CloudWatch Logs. When you create a rule, you are charged per-rule per-month, and for every million log events per month that match your rule.

components.text: The calculations below exclude Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Number of Contributor Insights rules for CloudWatch
Total number of matched log events for CloudWatch

options.label: million matched log events per hour
million matched log events per month

outputFrequency.label: million matched log events per month

components.label: price for insights rules for cloudwatch

operands.variableLabel: Number of Contributor Insights rules for CloudWatch
price for insights rules for cloudwatch
Total number of matched log events for CloudWatch

cards.title: Contributor Insights for DynamoDB
cards.helpText: CloudWatch Contributor Insights for DynamoDB is a diagnostic tool for identifying the most frequently accessed and throttled keys in your DynamoDB table at a glance. When you enable this feature, you are charged per-rule per-month and for every million DynamoDB Events, which occur when items are read from or written to your DynamoDB table.

components.text: The calculations below exclude Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Number of Contributor Insights rules for DynamoDB
Total number of events for DynamoDB
price for insights rules for DynamoDB

operands.variableLabel: Number of Contributor Insights rules for DynamoDB
price for insights rules for DynamoDB
Total number of events for DynamoDB

cards.title: Lambda Insights
cards.helpText: All CloudWatch metrics are prorated on an hourly basis. If your function is invoked less than once per hour, your function will only be billed for the hours that it is invoked. There are no minimum fees or mandatory service usage. If the function is not invoked, you do not pay.

components.text: The calculations below exclude Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Price for lambda metrics per month

components.tieredPricingReferLabel: Price for lambda metrics per month

cards.title: RUM Event
cards.helpText: All CloudWatch metrics are prorated on an hourly basis. If your function is invoked less than once per hour, your function will only be billed for the hours that it is invoked. There are no minimum fees or mandatory service usage. If the function is not invoked, you do not pay.

components.text: The calculations below exclude Free Tier discounts.

components.label: Monthly visitors to your web application

cards.title: Internet Monitor
cards.helpText: Amazon CloudWatch Internet Monitor helps you continually monitor internet availability and performance metrics between your Amazon Web Services-hosted applications and application end users. With Internet Monitor, you can quickly visualize the impact of issues, pinpoint locations and providers that are affected, and learn about actions you can take to improve your end users' network experience.

components.text: The calculations below are for a single monitor.

components.label: Number of city-networks to be monitored
components.placeholder: Enter number of city-networks to be monitored

components.text: * Internet Monitor publishes diagnostic logs to CloudWatch Logs for your top city-networks by traffic volume (up to 500 city-networks) every 5 minutes. You incur CloudWatch Logs charges for the logs. For more information, see the CloudWatch Logs pricing page.
